metadata.title: Write a CLAUDE.md Constitution for a Classroom Project with Claude Code
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)

then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

B09: palette=claude but the outro is 'NikBearBrownOutro' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration: One line in CLAUDE.md — no backend, mailto links only — and the next session never proposes a form-processing endpoint you can't deploy.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: This is Liam, in for Bear.

beats.narration: CLAUDE.md is not documentation. It is active context Claude reads at every session start. The cost of maintaining it is one line per constraint. The cost of not maintaining it is a session that re-derives all your decisions wrong.

beats.narration_text: Prompt design is the skill the platform doesn't want you to have, because a musician who can write a precise prompt is a musician who doesn't need to buy someone else's pre-packaged workflow. Every prompt in this video is a template you can adapt — paste it, swap the specifics, run it on your own catalog. The instruction is the tool.

artifactLines: The same tools the platforms use — pointed back at you.
Roll your own. Own the deck.

beats.narration: Ask Claude to generate a CLAUDE.md constitution for a class-website project with five explicit sections.

props.command:
claude "Generate a CLAUDE.md for a class-website project:
  Bash: deployment=rsync, no npm build step
  Style: semantic HTML5, vanilla CSS, no Tailwind
  Verification: npm run lint-html after HTML change
  Architecture: static-HTML only, no backend,
    contact forms are mailto: links
  Environment: school server old mod_rewrite,
    paths end in .html"

beats.narration: The generated CLAUDE.md has all five sections — Bash commands, style deviations, test runners, architecture constraints, and environment quirks.

props.code:
# CLAUDE.md — class-website

## Bash Commands
deploy: rsync -avz dist/ user@school:/var/www/
lint:   npm run lint-html

## Style Deviations
Semantic HTML5 only — no Tailwind, no Bootstrap
Vanilla CSS — no preprocessors

## Architecture
Static HTML only — NO backend, NO /api/* endpoints
Contact forms: mailto: links ONLY

## Environment Quirks
mod_rewrite active — all paths must end in .html
No trailing slash on any URL
props.title: CLAUDE.md

beats.narration: The generated CLAUDE.md has all five sections. The architecture section explicitly states no backend and no api endpoints. The environment section names mod_rewrite. A new session opened with this CLAUDE.md proposes a mailto link for the Contact page — not a form endpoint.
Remove the no-backend line and re-request a Contact page to see what changes.

props.command: claude "Remove the 'NO backend' line from CLAUDE.md.\nNow request a Contact page.\nWhat does Claude propose?"

beats.narration: With the no-backend line removed, Claude proposes a form endpoint with a Node.js handler. The same request, same model, same day — one line difference in CLAUDE.md, completely different proposal. The CLAUDE.md is not advice. It is constraint.

CLAUDE.md is a five-section constitution: Bash commands, style deviations, test runners, architectural decisions, environment quirks. Write it before the first session. Maintain it every time a new constraint is decided. It prevents every re-derivation.
Next: build a PreToolUse hook that makes grade generation mechanically impossible.
Like and subscribe for more Claude Code for Teachers.
beats.narration_text: Like and subscribe for more Claude Code for Teachers. From Musinique.
